text: Does a Gaussian wave packet v(x,0)=4*exp(-(x+5)^2/2.25), u(x,0)=0 decompose into a soliton train (>=2 well-separated peaks with amplitude >=0.8) under the coupled Burgers-swept-KdV system at T=6?

method: IMEX-CN spectral: CN on v_xxx (dispersive), explicit nonlinear (6vv_x + coupling) for v equation; spectral treatment for u with explicit nonlinear; Fourier pseudospectral with 2/3 dealiasing
bank_use_rationale: Adopting IMEX-CN spectral (kb-kdv-IMEX-CN-spectral-pass) for the dispersive v_xxx term; using 2/3 dealiasing and dt=0.0002 since amplitude=4 exceeds the [1,2] validated range (kb-gardner-KdV-method-transfer-moderate-amplitude); no negative bank entries available in PosOnly condition.

rationale: u equation blows up at t~1.1: u_max grows from 0 to 75 by t=1.0 as the Burgers component develops a shock. Pure spectral explicit treatment of the 3uu_x term violates CFL as u grows; need a TVD/upwind scheme for the u equation hyperbolic part.

Switch u equation to MUSCL-Godunov TVD scheme (kb-burgers-MUSCL-Godunov-shock-pass) for the hyperbolic 3uu_x term with adaptive CFL; keep IMEX-CN spectral for v equation. Use smaller sub-steps or adaptive dt for u to respect CFL when u is large.

method: Operator-split: v equation via IMEX-CN spectral (CN on v_xxx, explicit 6vv_x+d/dx(uv), 2/3 dealiasing, dt=0.002); u equation via MUSCL-van Leer+Godunov (CFL=0.45, adaptive substeps) with spectral source -d/dx(3v^2+v_xx)
bank_use_rationale: IMEX-CN spectral for v_xxx (kb-kdv-IMEX-CN-spectral-pass) retained; added MUSCL-Godunov with adaptive CFL for u Burgers part (kb-burgers-MUSCL-Godunov-shock-pass) to prevent CFL blow-up when u_max grows large.

rationale: 4 well-separated peaks with amplitudes [2.08, 0.87, 0.96, 1.16], all >= 0.8 threshold; mass conserved perfectly (0.00% drift); minimum peak separation ~2.8 (24 grid cells). Phenomenon target: >=2 peaks with amp>=0.8 and mass drift <8% — clearly met.
Phenomenon target fully satisfied: 4 peaks >= 0.8, mass drift 0.00% < 8%. Stopping early after iteration 2.
